step_by_step_thinking:
I agree with the previous conclusion that the special visceral afferent fibers of the facial nerve are located in the nucleus of the solitary tract. This is because these fibers carry taste sensations via the chorda tympani branch, and they also play a role in salivary gland control, both functions being integrated by the nucleus of the solitary tract.
```markdown. ## Answer Choice: C ```markdown.
raw_response:
I agree with the previous conclusion that the special visceral afferent fibers of the facial nerve are located in the nucleus of the solitary tract. This is because these fibers carry taste sensations via the chorda tympani branch, and they also play a role in salivary gland control, both functions being integrated by the nucleus of the solitary tract.
